column1: #   Each row maps one PharosIQ intent topic to its closest equivalent in the Bombora taxonomy.
#   Use the match_type column to prioritize your review effort before activating topics in a Bombora feed.
#   match_type = exact        -> Topic names matched exactly (case-insensitive). Safe to use as-is.

#   match_type = fuzzy_high   -> Score 90-99. The names are very similar but not identical (e.g. different punctuation or word order). Confirm the Bombora description aligns with your intent before activating.
#   match_type = fuzzy_medium -> Score 80-89. A plausible match but the concepts may differ in scope. Read the bombora_description and confirm it covers the same audience signal you expect.
#   match_type = fuzzy_low    -> Score 60-79. The algorithm found the closest available Bombora topic
column2: but it may be a partial match or a broader/narrower concept. Treat as a starting point for manual review.

column1: #   match_type = no_match     -> No Bombora topic scored above the minimum threshold. The PharosIQ topic may be proprietary
column3: or brand-specific. Consider requesting a custom Bombora topic or excluding it from the feed.

column1: #   match_score     -> Similarity score from 0 to 100. Higher is closer. Scores below 60 were excluded.
#   bombora_topic_id -> The unique Bombora topic ID to use when configuring intent segments or feeds in the Bombora platform.
#   bombora_description -> Bombora's own definition of the matched topic. Use this to confirm the match is semantically correct for your use case.
